name: Sierra de la Culebra Loop from Mahide
name_original: San Pedro de las Herrerías – vuelta por Mahide y Ferreras de Arriba
description: This 38.8-mile (62.5 km) moderate road cycling route in Sierra de la Culebra offers 2034 feet (620 m) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mahide traverse a landscape characterized by the Sierra de la Culebra mountain range, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features winding roads through hills and valleys, connecting rural villages. Cyclists can expect routes that navigate natural areas, including the vicinity of the Embalse de Cernadilla reservoir. The topography provides a mix of ascents and descents suitable for road biking. Best road cycling routes around Mahide The most popular road cycling route is Embalse de Cernadilla y Sierra de la Culebra — circular por Puebla de Sanabria, a 49.4 miles (79.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 58 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de la Culebra Loop from Mahide, a moderate 38.8 miles (62.5 km) path. This route provides a scenic loop through the Sierra de la Culebra with a balanced challenge. Local road cyclists also love the Sierra de la Culebra and Peña Mira Loop, a 44.8 miles (72.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Culebra, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Road cycling around Mahide is defined by the Sierra de la Culebra mountain range, rural villages, and the Embalse de Cernadilla reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy village loops to challenging mountain ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 130 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mahide's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Mahide?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Mahide, offering diverse terrain through the Sierra de la Culebra mountain range and rural villages. These routes range from easy village loops to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Mahide?
A: Yes, Mahide offers routes suitable for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368925090/from-ferreras-de-arriba-to-ferreras-de-abajo-circular-from-villardeciervos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Ferreras de Arriba to Ferreras de Abajo — circular from Villardeciervos&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 42.6 km route with a manageable elevation gain, perfect for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Mahide?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Mahide offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368839991/embalse-de-cernadilla-y-sierra-de-la-culebra-circular-por-puebla-de-sanabria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Cernadilla y Sierra de la Culebra — circular por Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 79.5 km route with over 1200 meters of elevation gain, traversing mountainous terrain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368702614/sierra-de-la-culebra-and-pena-mira-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Culebra and Peña Mira Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Mahide?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Mahide are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025465454/sierra-de-la-culebra-loop-from-mahide" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Culebra Loop from Mahide&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368711471/aliste-villages-loop-from-san-vitero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aliste Villages Loop from San Vitero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling around Mahide?
A: Road cycling around Mahide offers diverse landscapes, primarily defined by the Sierra de la Culebra mountain range. You'll encounter winding roads through hills and valleys, pass through charming rural villages, and ride in the vicinity of the Embalse de Cernadilla reservoir, providing a mix of natural beauty and cultural glimpses.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Mahide?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. The routes through the Sierra de la Culebra mountain range inherently provide stunning views. For a specific highlight, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mira Summit&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic vistas and is part of some challenging loops.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5457926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María de Mombuey&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fidionda Spring&lt;/a&gt; offers a natural point of interest. Some routes also pass through or near traditional settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5470458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanal de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Mahide?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, regions with mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Culebra typically offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, especially in valleys, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Embalse de Cernadilla reservoir?
A: Yes, the Embalse de Cernadilla reservoir is a prominent feature in the region. The challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368839991/embalse-de-cernadilla-y-sierra-de-la-culebra-circular-por-puebla-de-sanabria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Cernadilla y Sierra de la Culebra — circular por Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; route specifically includes the reservoir, offering beautiful waterside views during your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Mahide?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the varied terrain and scenic beauty of Mahide. Cyclists particularly enjoy the winding roads through the Sierra de la Culebra, the charm of the rural villages, and the balanced challenge offered by routes that combine ascents and descents. The region's natural areas and the Embalse de Cernadilla reservoir are also highly appreciated.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Mahide?
A: While road cycling (racebike) routes are generally geared towards more experienced riders, families looking for a less strenuous ride might consider the easier routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368925090/from-ferreras-de-arriba-to-ferreras-de-abajo-circular-from-villardeciervos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Ferreras de Arriba to Ferreras de Abajo — circular from Villardeciervos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that could be suitable for families with older children or those looking for a more leisurely pace.
tips: Villardeciervos is a small town located in the Sierra de la Culebra de Zamora. The town was declared a historical-artistic complex in 1987 due to its traditional constructions, most of them made of local stone.

Manzanal de Arriba is a small mountain town located at an altitude of 900 meters above sea level and located within the natural area of the Sierra de la Culebra natural hunting reserve. The area has been inhabited since the time of the Celts, as evidenced by the remains of stone settlements on the surrounding hills.

Nice town near Riofrío de Aliste in the province of Zamora. In Sarracín de Aliste we can stroll through the streets and visit the parish church or the typical and traditional buildings of the area. We can also stop to recover energy or take a break at the bakery or eat at one of its restaurants.
Pobladura de Aliste is a small town on the banks of the Aliste River in the Aliste las Laderas region. The town is located within the Natural Area of the Sierra de la Culebra.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: This racebike tour, named "San Pedro de las Herrerias - return via Mahide and Ferreras de Arriba", is approximately 62.5 km long. Komoot estimates the duration to be around 2 hours and 51 minutes, but this can vary based on your cycling speed and how often you stop.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The tour is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty. It features an elevation gain of about 620 meters, which suggests some climbs. While specific terrain details for this exact route are not widely available, a moderate rating for a racebike tour implies it's suitable for cyclists with some experience who are comfortable with varied inclines.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or villages along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several notable villages. You'll encounter Villardeciervos Village, Manzanal de Arriba, Sarracín de Aliste, and Pobladura de Aliste Village and Aliste River. These offer opportunities to experience local culture and scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal conditions for this exact route are not detailed, road cycling in the Zamora region of Spain is generally best enjoyed during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these periods, temperatures are typically milder and more comfortable than the intense heat of summer, and rainfall is usually moderate.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners on a racebike?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and over 600 meters of elevation gain, this route might be challenging for absolute beginners on a racebike. It's more suited for cyclists who have some experience with longer distances and climbs. If you're new to racebiking, you might consider shorter, flatter routes first.

faqs.question: Where can I park and what are the starting points for this tour?
faqs.answer: The tour is named "San Pedro de las Herrerias - return via Mahide and Ferreras de Arriba", suggesting San Pedro de las Herrerias as a primary starting point. While specific parking information for this exact trailhead isn't available, you would typically look for public parking within or on the outskirts of San Pedro de las Herrerias or one of the other villages along the route, such as Mahide or Ferreras de Arriba, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: As this is a racebike tour primarily on roads, dogs are generally not recommended to accompany cyclists due to safety concerns with traffic and the pace of cycling. If you are considering bringing a dog, it would need to be in a suitable trailer designed for cycling, and you should always prioritize their safety and comfort.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route in Sierra de la Culebra?
faqs.answer: This racebike tour is located within the Sierra de la Culebra region. Generally, for cycling on public roads and established routes in natural areas like Sierra de la Culebra, specific permits or entrance fees are not required. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected wildlife or private land. If you plan to deviate from public roads, it's advisable to check with local authorities.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for racebiking on this route?
faqs.answer: As a racebike tour, the route is expected to be predominantly on paved roads suitable for road bikes. While specific details about the road surface quality for this exact route are not readily available, the 'racebike' classification implies a focus on smooth, rideable surfaces. Some general information about other routes in the region suggests that some segments in certain areas might be unpaved, but for a dedicated racebike tour, paved roads are the standard expectation.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this racebike route briefly intersects with a couple of other paths. You will find yourself on sections of "Calle Melilla" for about 1% of the route and also on "Pista de Linarejos a San Pedro" for another 1%. These are minor intersections, and the tour primarily follows its own distinct path.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages including San Pedro de las Herrerias, Mahide, and Ferreras de Arriba, as well as Villardeciervos Village, Manzanal de Arriba, Sarracín de Aliste, and Pobladura de Aliste Village and Aliste River. These settlements are likely to offer opportunities for cafes, restaurants, or even accommodation, especially in the larger villages. It's always a good idea to check opening hours in advance, particularly in smaller communities.

waypoints.name: Pobladura de Aliste Village and Aliste River
